task_summary: Analyze the Sade-Feldman 2018 melanoma checkpoint-immunotherapy scRNA-seq dataset (GEO GSE120575) to identify cell populations that expand/contract with treatment, find marker genes, and derive candidate signatures that stratify responders from non-responders.

steps.title: Download GSE120575 data from GEO
steps.description: Fetch the supplementary files for GSE120575 from NCBI GEO: the TPM expression matrix (GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_TPM_GEO.txt.gz) and the per-cell metadata/patient-ID file (GSE120575_patient_ID_single_cells.txt.gz). Download in a dedicated fetch-only cell. Report file sizes and confirm integrity.

steps.title: Build annotated AnnData object
steps.description: Parse the TPM matrix (genes x 16,291 cells) and metadata into a scanpy AnnData. Populate obs with patient ID, sample, treatment timepoint (Pre/Post), response group (Responder/Non-responder), and therapy type. Validate label counts against the paper (e.g. ~17 responder / ~31 non-responder samples). Save as checkpoint data/sadefeldman.h5ad since re-downloading+parsing is slow. Create it in the scanpy env.

steps.title: QC, normalization, clustering, UMAP
steps.description: Run QC (mito %, genes/cell), work from the log2(TPM+1) values, select highly variable genes, scale, PCA, batch-aware neighbors, Leiden clustering, and UMAP. Deliverables: qc_metrics.png (violin/scatter of QC), umap_clusters.png colored by Leiden cluster. Save the processed object back to data/sadefeldman.h5ad (version update).

steps.title: Annotate clusters to immune cell types
steps.description: Score/inspect canonical CD45+ immune markers (CD8A/GZMB/PDCD1/HAVCR2 for exhausted CD8 T; IL7R/CCR7/TCF7/SELL for memory T; CD4/FOXP3 Treg; MS4A1/CD79A B cells; CD14/LYZ/CD68 monocyte-macrophage; FCER1A dendritic; NKG7/KLRD1 NK; MKI67 cycling). Assign cell-type labels per cluster. Deliverables: marker_dotplot.png and umap_celltypes.png (UMAP colored by annotated cell type).

steps.title: Compositional analysis: expansion/contraction
steps.description: Compute per-sample cell-type proportions and test which populations shift Pre->Post treatment and which differ Responder vs Non-responder (e.g. Wilcoxon on sample-level proportions, plus the CD8 memory-like vs exhausted ratio the paper highlights). Deliverables: composition_barplots.png (stacked proportions by timepoint and response), composition_boxplots.png (per-celltype proportion by group with p-values), and composition_stats.csv.

steps.title: Marker genes per population
steps.description: Run scanpy rank_genes_groups (Wilcoxon) to get marker genes for each annotated cell type. Deliverable: cluster_markers.csv (top markers per population with logFC and adjusted p) and a marker heatmap marker_heatmap.png.

steps.title: Responder-stratifying signature discovery
steps.description: Derive candidate signatures separating Responders from Non-responders: (a) pseudobulk/differential expression between R and NR (overall and within key populations such as CD8 T cells), (b) score the paper's reported responder markers (TCF7, SELL, CCR7, LTB, IGKC, PLAC8, LY9) and non-responder markers (HAVCR2, CD38, ENTPD1, CCL3, WARS) per cell, and (c) evaluate how well the memory/exhausted CD8 ratio and signature scores separate R vs NR at the sample level (ROC/AUC). Deliverables: responder_DE.csv, signature_scores.png (score distributions R vs NR), and signature_auc.png (ROC curves).

steps.title: Compile summary report
steps.description: Write summary_report.md synthesizing the dataset, cell-type landscape, populations that expand/contract with treatment and by response, top marker genes, and the candidate responder-stratifying signatures with their separation performance. Embed key figures. Per the user's instruction, reference raw data files >~1 MB (TPM matrix, h5ad) by name only, not as download links. List all saved artifacts.

feasibility.rationale: GSE120575 provides a processed TPM matrix (16,291 CD45+ immune cells, 48 biopsies) plus per-cell metadata including Pre/Post treatment and Responder/Non-responder labels — exactly the variables the task requires. All steps run locally with scanpy; the only risk is GEO download reliability, which is mitigated by checkpointing the assembled AnnData.
